metadata.title: AECT | AI Efficiency Control Tower
metadata.description: Intelligent pre-assessment layer for AI use-case intake

common.langSwitchConfirmBody: Your form entries will be lost.

apiErrors.caseNotFound: The case was not found. Please start the triage again.
apiErrors.notLoggedIn: Not signed in — please sign in as admin to perform this action.
apiErrors.adminNotConfigured: Server configuration incomplete: no admin access is set up on the backend.
apiErrors.alreadyProcessing: This submission is already being processed. Please wait a moment and try again.
apiErrors.tokenBudget: The hourly budget for language-model requests has been reached. Please wait briefly and try again.
apiErrors.internalError: Internal server error. Please try again later.
apiErrors.status404: The requested resource was not found.
apiErrors.status409: Conflict: the request collides with an ongoing operation.
apiErrors.status422: The input is invalid or incomplete.
apiErrors.status429: Too many requests — please wait briefly and try again.
apiErrors.status5xx: Server error. Please try again later.
apiErrors.timeout: Timeout: the server did not respond in time. Please try again.
apiErrors.connectionFailed: Connection to the backend failed. Is the API server running (port 8000)?
apiErrors.aiUnavailable: The AI service is currently unavailable — please try again later.
apiErrors.loginNotConfigured: Admin login is not set up on the server.
apiErrors.tooManyLogins: Too many login attempts — please wait briefly and try again.
apiErrors.noSessionToken: Login response without a session token.

footer.disclaimer: This application uses an AI system (Azure OpenAI). All outputs are non-binding guidance for expert review, not a legally binding decision.

landing.heroTitle: From idea to a well-founded decision.
landing.heroLead: Got an idea where AI would genuinely save time? Describe it in a few sentences — we'll work out the value, effort and risk.
landing.pipelineCaption: Clear rules check the facts, vetted sources provide the evidence, a language model turns it into plain text — a person makes the final call.
landing.cardSubmitDesc: A few steps, and your idea gets a reasoned assessment instead of a gut feeling.
landing.cardIdeasDesc: No finished idea yet? Describe your problem — the assistant turns it into drafts.
landing.cardCasesDesc: See what others submitted and where it stands — before you do the work twice.
landing.cardBoardDesc: Portfolio matrix: benefit against feasibility, each case a point.
landing.cardMonitoringDesc: Maintain approved and implemented use cases — status and progress notes.
landing.adminSuffix: — unlocks Board, Monitoring and the editing actions.

login.title: Sign in
login.intro: Admin sign-in. Please sign in with the admin password.

errors.adoption: Please select the commitment of use.
errors.data: Please select the data protection class.

confirm.title: Use case submitted
confirm.next: Next, the AI Board assesses the case: benefit, effort and data protection are reviewed, and the result appears on the case detail page.

fields.titlePlaceholder: Short, concise title of the use case
fields.currentStatePlaceholder: How does the process work today?
fields.desiredStatePlaceholder: How should the process work in the future?
fields.exampleHelp: Describe how this process or task actually runs today — step by step.
fields.desiredExamplePlaceholder: How the same task should look after AI is used
fields.desiredExampleHelp: Optional — how the task should look after AI is used.
fields.timeCurrentHelp: How many hours does the task take today — once, without AI? (0.5 = 30 minutes)
fields.timeAiHelp: How many hours would the same task take with the AI solution, roughly?
fields.occurrencesHelp: How often does ONE employee perform this task per year?
fields.employeesHelp: How many employees perform this task regularly?
fields.piiHelp: Tick this if the task involves data about individual people (e.g. names, customer or employee records). If that applies together with “Regulatory pressure” below, AECT recommends that a human reviews the case before implementation.
fields.currentStateHelp: Describe the process as it really is today — including the points where it gets stuck. For example: “Requests arrive by email and are sorted by hand.”
fields.desiredStateHelp: Describe what should be different in the end — the outcome, not the technology. For example: “Requests are pre-sorted and land directly with the right team.”
fields.employeeCategoryHelp: Which level do the majority of the colleagues who are meant to use the solution have?

approach.simple_integration: Connecting into an existing environment, minimal development effort.
approach.development_on_existing: Development/extension on an already existing platform.
approach.api_integration: Integrate external services via their interface (API).
approach.new_tool: Introduce a new tool or platform (high effort).

data.pseudonymous: Personal reference only reconstructable with extra info (Art. 4(5) GDPR) — stays GDPR-relevant.
data.personal: Directly attributable to a person (Art. 4(1) GDPR).
data.sensitive_personal: Health, origin, biometrics etc. — highest protection level (Art. 9 GDPR).

evidence.pure_estimate: Gut feeling without a data basis.
evidence.similar_project: Own experience or a comparable project.
evidence.tested_piloted: Tested or measured with real examples.

adoption.voluntary: Opt-in — nobody is required to use it.

pressure.strategicHint: Part of the corporate strategy or a board objective.

review.intro: Please review your entries. After submitting, AECT assesses the use case; you see the result on the case detail page.

sectionIntro.idee: This is where you describe what it is about: who is submitting, how the process works today and how it should work in future. These texts are the basis for the qualitative assessment.
sectionIntro.menge: This is where you capture how much time the task costs and how often it occurs. AECT uses this to calculate the possible time saved and the benefit in euros.
sectionIntro.umsetzung: This is where you estimate how the solution would be built and what it costs. That gives the effort which is weighed against the benefit.
sectionIntro.daten: This is where you classify which data is involved and how binding the use should be. It drives data protection notes and the reliability of the estimate.
sectionIntro.pruefen: This is where you see all your entries again at a glance. Nothing has been saved yet — only the submit button files the use case.

scoring.title: How the point system works
scoring.intro: The effort score adds up three dimensions. The higher the total, the more demanding or risky the implementation.

complexity.label: Complexity
complexity.text: Follows directly from the chosen implementation approach: simple integration 1, development on existing systems 2, API integration 3, custom development 4, new tool 5.

cost.label: Cost
cost.text: One point each for license and implementation cost once they reach the configured cost threshold — otherwise no point. The applicable threshold appears in the cost rationale below.

dataProtection.label: Data protection
dataProtection.text: No personal data 0 points; personal or pseudonymous 1 point (pseudonymous data remains personal, Art. 4(5) GDPR); special categories under Art. 9 GDPR 2 points.

scoring.total: Total 1 to 9: up to 3 the effort counts as low, up to 6 as moderate, above that as high. Feasibility is its counterpart (10 minus the effort score).
scoring.zoneEffect: The zone comes from expected benefit and effort score together — high benefit at low effort gives the best classification. Stated urgency (regulatory, competitive, strategic) can then upgrade the zone. The recommendation (AI, automation, expert review) is derived separately from the detected criteria — it appears above with its rationale.

caseTools.title: Admin tools
caseTools.llmHint: Refine and Propose Solution are AI actions · 5–30 seconds.
caseTools.complianceHint: RAG-backed guidance (EU AI Act Art. 50, GDPR Art. 35 where relevant) · AI action.
caseTools.complianceNone: No specific compliance guidance for this case.

decision.analysisRunning: Analysis running — decision possible once it has finished.

sharpening.title: Refinement draft — please review
sharpening.helpDiff: Green = newly proposed, struck through = removed. The draft is only saved after “Accept”.
sharpening.helpAutoSplit: Strong rewrite detected — side-by-side view is preset.
sharpening.actionError: The action could not be performed.

solution.title: Solution proposal
solution.description: AI draft, to be reviewed. “Accept” saves both variants, “Discard” saves nothing.
solution.confirmDiscard: Discard proposal? The draft will be lost and would have to be regenerated.

monitoring.pageLead: Maintain the status directly and document the history per use case as an append-only timeline — no automatic tracking.
monitoring.loadErrorFallback: The list could not be loaded.
monitoring.empty: No approved or implemented use cases yet. Approvals happen in the report on the case detail page.
monitoring.entriesLoadError: The entries could not be loaded.
monitoring.notePlaceholder: Note an observation, progress or decision …
monitoring.noteSaveError: The entry could not be saved.
monitoring.discontinueTooltip: Marks the use case as no longer actively monitored — the lifecycle status stays unchanged.
monitoring.reinstateTooltip: Clears the "discontinued" mark — the use case counts as actively monitored again.
monitoring.discontinueError: The use case could not be marked as discontinued.
monitoring.reinstateError: The use case could not be reinstated.
monitoring.discontinueDialogDesc: The use case will be marked as no longer actively monitored. Reason and name are recorded in the history.
monitoring.reinstateDialogDesc: The use case counts as actively monitored again. Reason and name are recorded in the history.
monitoring.reasonPlaceholder: Why is the use case being discontinued or reactivated?
monitoring.actorLabel: Name of the person performing this

board.pageLead: Each assessed use case as a point: expected net benefit (x), feasibility (y, inverted effort score) and hours saved per year (bubble size). Color shows the triage zone. Click a point to open the case.
board.loadErrorFallback: The list could not be loaded.
board.emptyFiltered: No assessed use cases for this filter.
board.unscored: {count, plural, one {# case} other {# cases}} without assessment (prefilter not passed).
board.xAxisDesc: Expected net benefit per year — theoretical potential x usage factor x evidence factor, minus license cost.
board.yAxisDesc: Feasibility — the effort score (complexity 1–5 + cost 0–2 + data protection 0–2) inverted: the higher the point, the lower the implementation effort.
board.bubbleDesc: Size = work hours saved per year.

ideation.title: From problem to draft
ideation.lead: Describe a problem or an inefficient process — the assistant turns it into 1 to 3 use-case drafts for submission. Drafts deliberately contain no numbers: you add quantities, times and evidence at submission.
ideation.placeholder: e.g. Inbox requests are categorized and forwarded manually; this takes a long time and is error-prone.
ideation.hint: The more concrete the problem, the stronger the drafts.
ideation.error: The drafts could not be generated. Please try again.
ideation.flagged: The input contained patterns that look like instructions to the system — they were treated as data.
ideation.emptyResult: No drafts could be generated for this description. Please phrase the problem a bit more concretely.

cases.pageLead: The entire portfolio at a glance: filter by status and zone, sort by net benefit or submission date, set the lifecycle status right in the row.
cases.loadErrorFallback: The list could not be loaded.
cases.emptyFiltered: No use cases match the filters.
cases.count: {count, plural, one {# use case} other {# use cases}} · {filtered} filtered
cases.pageLeadPublic: All submitted use cases at a glance: filter by status, sort by submission date.

similarCases.title: Similar use cases
similarCases.footer: Similarity is based on text embeddings of title and current-state description — not a judgment of content.

caseInputs.title: Captured inputs
caseInputs.piiEffect: Together with regulatory pressure, this entry causes the case to be reviewed by experts before implementation and a data protection impact assessment to be recommended. It does not affect the effort score — that is driven by the data protection class.

implApproach.reevalHint: Adding this triggers a full re-assessment of the case.

sketch.renderFailed: The sketch could not be drawn — here is the underlying Mermaid source.
sketch.confirmRegen: The existing sketch will be replaced by a new draft. Continue?
sketch.generatedAt: Generated on {date} · AI-generated draft — to be reviewed, not a binding target picture.
sketch.noProposal: An architecture sketch needs an accepted solution proposal as a basis — there is none for this use case.
sketch.noProposalTooltip: First generate and accept a solution proposal.
sketch.intro: A generic architecture sketch from the solution proposal — five building-block types as a draft, not a binding target picture.
sketch.hint: Takes a few seconds · AI call

detailPage.notFoundBody: There is no entry for the requested case {id}. It may have been deleted.
detailPage.loadError: The assessment could not be loaded.
detailPage.pendingBody: No implementation approach has been chosen for this case yet. Once an admin adds it above, the case is fully assessed (benefit, effort, risk).
detailPage.reviewingHeading: Under review by the AI Board
detailPage.reviewingBody: Submitted on {date}. The AI Board's decision appears here once it has been made.
detailPage.decisionBlockedBody: Without an implementation approach the case is not assessed — there is no benefit, effort or recommendation for the AI Board to decide on. Once the approach is added, the case is assessed automatically and the decision becomes possible.
detailPage.decisionBlockedLink: Add the implementation approach in the use case above

rationale.sentence: Of the theoretical potential ({potential}), <netTag>{net}</netTag> remains: usage <usageTag>{usage}</usageTag> × evidence <evidenceTag>{evidence}</evidenceTag>.
rationale.sentenceLicense: Of the theoretical potential ({potential}), <netTag>{net}</netTag> remains: usage <usageTag>{usage}</usageTag> × evidence <evidenceTag>{evidence}</evidenceTag>, minus {license} license cost.

status.evaluationPendingTooltip: No implementation approach provided — evaluation follows once an admin adds it.

LIKELY_WIN.label: Promising
LIKELY_WIN.summary: Clear impact lever, low implementation risk.

CALCULATED_RISK.label: Calculated risk
CALCULATED_RISK.summary: Viable, but tied to conditions and effort.

MARGINAL_GAIN.label: Marginal gain
MARGINAL_GAIN.summary: Benefit is out of proportion to the effort.

contact.hint: Questions about a submission or the process? Get in touch.

boardDecision.noRationale: The AI Board did not record a rationale.
